text:
MOLECULAR PATHOLOGY / COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT

INSTITUTION: Department of Molecular Pathology & Genomic Medicine
DIVISION: Oncogenomics Laboratory — Solid Tumor Panel

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Patient Name:          Delacroix, Robert
Date of Birth:         [on file]
Sex:                   Male
MRN:                   [on file]
Ordering Physician:    Medical Oncology
Diagnosis (submitted): Metastatic castration-resistant prostatic adenocarcinoma, visceral progression

Specimen ID:           SP-MLT-2024-07841
Specimen Type:         Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core needle biopsy tissue
Anatomic Site:         Liver, Segment 5 (CT-guided)
Date of Procedure:     [see header]
Date Received:         [see header]
Date Reported:         [see header]
Tumor Cellularity:     Estimated 65% (pathologist-reviewed H&E, adequate for sequencing)
Tissue QC:             PASS — DNA yield 320 ng, QC score 8.4/10; RNA yield 180 ng (sufficient for fusion/splice variant analysis)

Comparator Sample:     Germline reference — peripheral blood (previously tested; BRCA2 germline variant c.5946delT confirmed, archived report SP-GML-2021-00342)

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
METHODOLOGY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Platform:              Illumina NovaSeq 6000
Panel:                 Comprehensive Solid Tumor DNA + RNA Panel (525-gene panel with copy number and fusion analysis)
Coverage:              Mean sequencing depth 742x (DNA); 80x (RNA)
Minimum VAF threshold: 3% (somatic SNVs/indels); 20 copies (amplifications)
Variant filtering:     Somatic variant calling performed with paired germline subtraction using archived blood-based germline reference
RNA-based assay:       Yes — splice variant and AR transcript analysis by targeted RNA sequencing
Biomarkers assessed:   SNVs, indels, copy number alterations (CNAs), structural rearrangements/fusions, MSI, TMB, AR splice variants (including ARv7), PD-L1 expression (reported separately by IHC, see pathology report SP-BIO-2024-07841)
Germline interpretation: Germline variants reported separately; this report incorporates archived germline context for LOH analysis only

Note: This analysis was performed on the liver metastasis specimen to evaluate acquired resistance mechanisms following progression on ola (PARP inhibitor) therapy. Comparison to prior primary tumor NGS (prostate biopsy specimen, SP-NGS-2021-00344) is provided where applicable.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SOMATC FINDINGS — PATHOGENIC / LIKELY PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

SNVs and Small Indels
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Gene       Exon/Region    Nucleotide Change        Amino Acid Change     VAF    Classification
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TP53       Exon 7         c.742C>T                 p.Arg248Trp           58%    Pathogenic
                           [PRESENT IN PRIMARY, VAF 42% — enriched in metastasis]
CDK12      Exon 28        c.3637_3638delAA         p.Lys1213fsTer6       34%    Pathogenic
                           [PRESENT IN PRIMARY, VAF 18% — enriched in metastasis]
RB1        Intron 6       c.607-2A>G               Splice acceptor        45%    Pathogenic
                           [PRESENT IN PRIMARY, VAF 31% — enriched in metastasis]
PIK3CA     Exon 9         c.1633G>A                p.Glu545Lys           22%    Pathogenic
                           [PRESENT IN PRIMARY, VAF 12% — enriched in metastasis]
AKT1       Exon 3         c.49G>A                  p.Glu17Lys             8%    Pathogenic
                           [NOT DETECTED IN PRIMARY — acquired/newly detected]
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Copy Number Alterations
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Gene       Alteration               Copy Number     Prior (Primary)    Classification
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AR         High-level amplification    14             8                  Pathogenic / Resistance
MYC        Amplification               16             12                 Pathogenic
MET        High-level amplification    18 (new)       Not detected       Pathogenic / Resistance
PTEN       Homozygous deletion          0              0                  Pathogenic
BRCA2      Heterozygous deletion*       1              1 (germline het.)  LOH confirmed
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
*BRCA2 somatic loss of heterozygosity (LOH) confirmed, consistent with biallelic inactivation in tumor. Germline pathogenic variant c.5946delT (p.Ser1982Argfs*22) confirmed from archived germline reference. See prior germline report SP-GML-2021-00342.

AR Splice Variant Analysis (RNA-based)
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Variant              Detection Method         Result          Prior (Primary)
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AR-V7 (AR exon 3b    Targeted RNA-seq         POSITIVE        Not detected
  splice variant)    + RT-PCR confirmatory    (Confirmed)     (Not assayed on primary)
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AR-V7 encodes a constitutively active, ligand-binding domain-truncated androgen receptor isoform. AR-V7 positivity is associated with resistance to androgen receptor signaling inhibitors (enza, Erleada, darolutamide, Zytiga).

Fusions / Structural Rearrangements
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Fusion Partner(s)      Read-through Pairs     Quality Score     Result
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ERG, ETV1, ETV4,
FLI1, RAF1, ROS1,
NTRK1/2/3, ALK,
RET, FGFR1-3         N/A                     N/A               NOT DETECTED
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
No oncogenic gene fusions identified.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN & MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):       6 mutations/Mb    [Low; threshold for high: ≥10 mut/Mb]
Microsatellite Instability (MSI):    MSS (Microsatellite Stable)
                                      [30 loci evaluated; 0/30 unstable]
HRD Score:                           High [Homologous Recombination Deficiency confirmed by genomic scars — LOH fraction 38%, telomeric allelic imbalance score 16, large-scale state transitions 14; composite HRD score 68 (threshold >42 = HRD-positive)]

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Gene       Alteration              VAF    Comment
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
FANCA      p.Ile1097Val            6%     VUS; no prior evidence of pathogenicity in prostate cancer context; may represent sub-clonal acquired alteration
ATM        c.2572A>G (p.Thr858Ala) 4%     VUS; low VAF, likely sub-clonal; ATM hotspot not affected; germline ATM previously negative
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
NEGATIVE / NOT DETECTED FINDINGS (SELECTED CLINICALLY RELEVANT GENES)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Gene(s) assessed and NOT detected as altered:
BRCA1 (somatic), PALB2, CDH1, CHEK2, BARD1, RAD51C, RAD51D, NBN, MLH1, MSH2, MSH6, PMS2, POLE (exonuclease domain), KRAS, NRAS, BRAF V600E, EGFR, ALK, RET, FGFR2, FGFR3, IDH1, IDH2, CDKN2A, SMAD4, ARID1A, VHL, KIT, PDGFRA, NF1, STK11

AR point mutations (LBD): AR p.L702H, p.W742C, p.H875Y, p.T878A — NOT DETECTED (resistance driven by ARv7 splice variant and AR amplification rather than LBD point mutation)

Neuroendocrine marker assessment by IHC (reported in companion pathology report SP-BIO-2024-07841): Synaptophysin NEGATIVE, Chromogranin NEGATIVE, INSM1 NEGATIVE — no evidence of neuroendocrine transdifferentiation

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CLINICAL INTERPRETATION
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
This comprehensive genomic profile was obtained from a liver metastasis biopsy at a time of documented radiographic and biochemical progression following ola (PARP inhibitor) therapy. Comparison with the previously reported prostate primary biopsy NGS profile reveals clonal evolution and acquisition of several therapeutically relevant alterations:

1. AR-V7 SPLICE VARIANT (NEW; not detected on primary biopsy): Detection of the androgen receptor variant 7 (ARv7) indicates a constitutively active truncated AR isoform lacking the ligand-binding domain. This is a well-established mechanism of resistance to both first- and second-generation androgen receptor signaling inhibitors (ARSIs), including enza and Zytiga. ARv7 positivity is correlated with poor response to further ARSI therapy. Taxane-based chemotherapy (doce or cabazitaxel) retains activity in ARv7-positive disease.

2. MET HIGH-LEVEL AMPLIFICATION (NEW; copy number 18): High-level MET amplification represents a newly acquired genomic alteration not present in the prostate primary specimen. This is a recognized acquired resistance mechanism in advanced prostate cancer and may contribute to ola resistance and ARSI-refractory disease. MET amplification may potentially confer sensitivity to MET-targeted therapies, though clinical data in prostate cancer remain limited.

3. AKT1 p.E17K MUTATION (NEW; VAF 8%): This sub-clonal AKT1 activating mutation at the pleckstrin homology domain results in constitutive PI3K-AKT pathway activation. Combined with the pre-existing PIK3CA p.E545K mutation and PTEN homozygous deletion (already present in the primary), this tumor harbors convergent activation of the PI3K-AKT pathway across multiple genomic nodes. The low VAF (8%) suggests this is a sub-clonal acquired event, possibly representing an emerging resistant clone under selective pressure.

4. BRCA2 BIALLELIC LOSS (CONFIRMED; germline + somatic LOH): Biallelic inactivation of BRCA2 is maintained from the primary tumor and confirmed in this metastatic deposit. BRCA2 biallelic loss should confer sensitivity to PARP inhibitors; however, disease is progressing on Lynparza, suggesting additional resistance mechanisms have supervened (ARv7, MET amplification, AKT1 pathway). Notably, no BRCA2 reversion mutations were identified in this specimen — ola resistance in this case does not appear to be driven by BRCA2 restoration.

5. CDK12 BIALLELIC INACTIVATION: CDK12 p.K1213fs*6 (VAF 34%) is enriched from the primary (VAF 18%), consistent with clonal expansion. CDK12 biallelic inactivation in prostate cancer has been associated with focal tandem duplications (FTDs) genome-wide, and a subset of CDK12-deficient prostate cancers may exhibit a distinct mutational signature (tandem duplications rather than high somatic TMB). CDK12 biallelic inactivation has been proposed as a potential predictor of benefit from immunotherapy combinations in some preliminary reports, although TMB remains low (6 mut/Mb) and MSI status is stable (MSS) in this case.

6. TP53 p.R248W, RB1 SPLICE VARIANT, PIK3CA p.E545K, AR AMPLIFICATION, PTEN DELETION, MYC AMPLIFICATION: All clonal alterations present in the primary tumor are retained and enriched (higher VAFs) in the metastatic deposit, consistent with their founding clonal status and ongoing selective pressure. AR amplification has increased from copy number 8 to 14, further supporting AR-driven resistance.

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
ACTIONABILITY SUMMARY — TIER CLASSIFICATION (AMP/ASCO/CAP 2017)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TIER I — Strong Clinical Significance (FDA-approved therapy exists)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Biomarker            Alteration         Implication                          Agent(s)
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
BRCA2                Biallelic loss     Sensitivity — PARP inhibitor         ola (Lynparza), Rubraca
                     (germline + LOH)   [Patient currently on/progressing     Zejula (in combination)
                                         Lynparza; reversion mutation absent]
AR-V7                Splice variant     Resistance — ARSIs                   Taxane chemotherapy preferred
                     (RNA-positive)     (abi, enza, etc.)     (doce, cabazitaxel)
PIK3CA p.E545K       Activating SNV     Sensitivity — PI3K/AKT inhibitor     Idelalisib (not prostate-labeled);
  + PTEN deletion    + CNA              [Convergent PI3K-AKT activation]     investigational in prostate cancer
TMB-Low (6/Mb)       N/A               Less likely to benefit from          pembro (Keytruda) — less
                                        Keytruda monotherapy             likely to respond on TMB alone
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TIER II — Potential Clinical Significance (Emerging evidence / off-label / clinical trial)
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Biomarker            Alteration         Implication                          Context
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
MET                  High-level         Potential sensitivity — MET          MET inhibitors (cabozantinib has
                     amplification      inhibitors                           anti-MET activity; savolitinib,
                     (CN 18)                                                 tepotinib); clinical data in
                                                                              prostate cancer limited
CDK12                Biallelic          Possible immunotherapy benefit        CDK12 loss-associated FTD
                     inactivation       (in combination); tumor             signature; clinical trials
                                        neoantigen hypothesis                 exploring immune combinations
AKT1 p.E17K          Activating SNV     Potential AKT inhibitor              Capivasertib + doce or
  (sub-clonal)                          sensitivity                          ipatasertib combinations;
                                                                              sub-clonal — uncertain relevance
HRD-positive         Composite HRD      May retain some HR-directed          Alternative PARP inhibitors;
                     score 68           therapy benefit                       combination strategies
───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

TIER III — Unknown Clinical Significance
─────────────────────────────────────────
ATM p.Thr858Ala (VUS; low VAF), FANCA p.Ile1097Val (VUS; sub-clonal)

TIER IV — Benign / Likely Benign: None identified

─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
SUMMARY STATEMENT
─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
Comprehensive genomic profiling of this hepatic metastasis from a patient with heavily pre-treated castration-resistant prostatic adenocarcinoma identifies a complex, clonally evolved tumor genome. The primary clonal architecture (TP53, PTEN, CDK12, RB1, PIK3CA, AR amplification, MYC amplification) is maintained and expanded from the initial prostate primary. Critically, this metastatic specimen demonstrates three newly acquired or enriched resistance-associated alterations: (1) AR-V7 splice variant positivity, predicting resistance to ARSI-based therapies; (2) high-level MET amplification (CN 18), a recognized acquired resistance mechanism; and (3) a sub-clonal AKT1 p.E17K activating mutation, adding convergent PI3K-AKT pathway activation. BRCA2 biallelic loss is confirmed without evidence of reversion mutation, indicating that Lynparza resistance in this case is not attributable to BRCA2 restoration. The overall genomic landscape is consistent with a highly aggressive, genomically complex CRPC with multiple co-occurring resistance mechanisms.

These findings have been reviewed by the molecular tumor board. Clinical correlation and multidisciplinary discussion are warranted. Clinical trial enrollment should be considered.

Reported by: [Molecular Pathologist, MD/PhD — Molecular Pathology & Genomic Medicine]
Verified by:  [Director, Oncogenomics Laboratory]
Report status: FINAL

For questions regarding this report, please contact the Oncogenomics Laboratory at extension [on file].

This report is intended for use by qualified healthcare professionals in the context of the individual patient's clinical circumstances. Genomic findings should be interpreted in conjunction with clinical, pathological, and imaging data.
